Question: Evaluate using the properties of logarithms: ⓐ $$\text{log}_{13} 1$$ ⓑ $$\text{log}_{9} 9 .$$
Evaluate using the properties of logarithms: ⓐ $$\text{log}_{5} 1$$ ⓑ $$\text{log}_{7} 7 .$$
Evaluate using the properties of logarithms: ⓐ $$5^{\text{log}_{5} 15}$$ ⓑ $$\text{log}_{7} 7^{4} .$$
Evaluate using the properties of logarithms: ⓐ $$2^{\text{log}_{2} 8}$$ ⓑ $$\text{log}_{2} 2^{15} .$$
Use the Product Property of Logarithms to write each logarithm as a sum of logarithms. Simplify, if possible. ⓐ $$\text{log}_{3} 3 x$$ ⓑ $$\text{log}_{2} 8 x y$$
Use the Product Property of Logarithms to write each logarithm as a sum of logarithms. Simplify, if possible. ⓐ $$\text{log}_{9} 9 x$$ ⓑ $$\text{log}_{3} 27 x y$$
Use the Quotient Property of Logarithms to write each logarithm as a difference of logarithms. Simplify, if possible. ⓐ $$\text{log}_{4} \frac{3}{4}$$ ⓑ $$\text{log} \frac{x}{1000}$$
Use the Quotient Property of Logarithms to write each logarithm as a difference of logarithms. Simplify, if possible. ⓐ $$\text{log}_{2} \frac{5}{4}$$ ⓑ $$\text{log} \frac{10}{y}$$
Use the Power Property of Logarithms to write each logarithm as a product of logarithms. Simplify, if possible. ⓐ $$\text{log}_{7} 5^{4}$$ ⓑ $$\text{log} x^{100}$$
Use the Power Property of Logarithms to write each logarithm as a product of logarithms. Simplify, if possible. ⓐ $$\text{log}_{2} 3^{7}$$ ⓑ $$\text{log} x^{20}$$
Use the Properties of Logarithms to expand the logarithm $$\text{log}_{2} ( 5 x^{4} y^{2} )$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Use the Properties of Logarithms to expand the logarithm $$\text{log}_{3} ( 7 x^{5} y^{3} )$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Use the Properties of Logarithms to expand the logarithm $$\text{log}_{4} \sqrt[5]{\frac{x^{4}}{2 y^{3} z^{2}}}$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Use the Properties of Logarithms to expand the logarithm $$\text{log}_{3} \sqrt[3]{\frac{x^{2}}{5 y^{} z}}$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Use the Properties of Logarithms to condense the logarithm $$\text{log}_{2} 5 + \text{log}_{2} x - \text{log}_{2} y$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Use the Properties of Logarithms to condense the logarithm $$\text{log}_{3} 6 - \text{log}_{3} x - \text{log}_{3} y$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Use the Properties of Logarithms to condense the logarithm $$3 \text{log}_{2} x + 2 \text{log}_{2} ( x - 1 )$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Use the Properties of Logarithms to condense the logarithm $$2 \text{log} x + 2 \text{log} ( x + 1 )$$ . Simplify, if possible.
Rounding to three decimal places, approximate $$\text{log}_{3} 42 .$$
Rounding to three decimal places, approximate $$\text{log}_{5} 46 .$$
